pullRequest.title: [DOM-74661] Add create, patch, and delete Extension audit events

nodes.body: Will update before merging this PR once https://github.com/cerebrotech/idsm-audit-trail-openapi-library/pull/119 is merged.

```shell
#!/bin/bash
# Read-only verification: check whether this branch-scoped version is uniquely pinned
# and whether similar non-release pins exist in lockfiles.

set -euo pipefail

echo "== Exact pin occurrences =="
rg -n --no-heading 'com\.dominodatalab:audit-trail-openapi_2\.13|0\.0\.0-ddl-eric-jin-DOM-74661-add-extensions-audit' 3rdparty/maven_install.json

echo
echo "== Other branch-scoped/internal pre-release pins in Maven lockfiles =="
fd 'maven_install\.json$' 3rdparty -x rg -n --no-heading '0\.0\.0-[A-Za-z0-9._-]+' {}

echo
echo "== Current pinned version for this artifact in this lockfile =="
python - <<'PY'
import json
with open("3rdparty/maven_install.json") as f:
    data = json.load(f)
entry = data["artifacts"].get("com.dominodatalab:audit-trail-openapi_2.13")
print(entry)
PY
```

**Promote branch-scoped artifact pin to stable version before merging.**

The artifact `com.dominodatalab:audit-trail-openapi_2.13` is pinned to `0.0.0-ddl-eric-jin-DOM-74661-add-extensions-audit`, a branch-scoped development version. This is the only pre-release pin of this type in the lockfile and reduces reproducibility across environments. Either promote to a stable published version before merge or document this as a temporary integration pin with explicit completion criteria.

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed.

In `@3rdparty/maven_install.json` around lines 541 - 545, The lockfile pins the
artifact com.dominodatalab:audit-trail-openapi_2.13 to a branch-scoped
pre-release version "0.0.0-ddl-eric-jin-DOM-74661-add-extensions-audit"; update
the pin to a stable released version or explicitly document this as a temporary
integration pin with completion criteria. Locate the entry for
com.dominodatalab:audit-trail-openapi_2.13 in the 3rdparty/maven_install.json
lock content and replace the version string
"0.0.0-ddl-eric-jin-DOM-74661-add-extensions-audit" with the appropriate
released semantic version, or add a clear comment/metadata in your repo/docs
describing why the branch-scoped pin is allowed and when it will be replaced by
a stable release.
```

</details>

**Development version must be replaced with a stable semantic version before merging.**

The dependency version `0.0.0-ddl-eric-jin-DOM-74661-add-extensions-audit` is a development/feature branch version and not a proper semantic version. This blocks merging as it breaks reproducible builds and violates semantic versioning conventions.

Update to the stable version `0.0.68` (or newer if available from upstream) before final merge. The upstream repository appears to be private, so verify the latest stable release directly with the maintainers if a newer version is required.

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```

`0.0.0-ddl-eric-jin-DOM-74661-add-extensions-audit` in dependencies.yaml with a
stable semantic version (use `0.0.68` or a newer upstream release); locate the
version entry that currently holds that exact string and update it to the stable
semantic version, and if necessary confirm the latest available stable release
with the upstream maintainers before changing to a newer number.
```

</details>

_⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟠 Major_

**Guard post-write audit calls to avoid false operation failures.**

At **Line 64**, **Line 139**, and **Line 155**, telemetry is called after persistence without protection. If event recording fails, the write has already succeeded but the API can return an error, which can trigger unsafe retries on create/patch/delete.


<details>
<summary>🛠️ Suggested fail-open wrapper for audit emission</summary>

```diff
+import scala.util.control.NonFatal
@@
-    extensionEventTracker.recordCreateExtension(principal, extension.id, extension.name, extension.appId)
+    recordAuditSafely(s"create extension ${extension.id}") {
+      extensionEventTracker.recordCreateExtension(principal, extension.id, extension.name, extension.appId)
+    }
@@
-    extensionEventTracker.recordPatchExtension(principal, patchedExtension.id, patchedExtension.name, patchedExtension.appId, dto)
+    recordAuditSafely(s"patch extension ${patchedExtension.id}") {
+      extensionEventTracker.recordPatchExtension(principal, patchedExtension.id, patchedExtension.name, patchedExtension.appId, dto)
+    }
@@
-      extensionEventTracker.recordDeleteExtension(principal, deletedExtension.id, deletedExtension.name, deletedExtension.appId)
+      recordAuditSafely(s"delete extension ${deletedExtension.id}") {
+        extensionEventTracker.recordDeleteExtension(principal, deletedExtension.id, deletedExtension.name, deletedExtension.appId)
+      }
@@
+  private def recordAuditSafely(action: String)(f: => Unit): Unit = {
+    try f
+    catch {
+      case NonFatal(e) =>
+        logger.warn(s"Audit event emission failed for $action", e)
+    }
+  }
```
</details>


Also applies to: 128-140, 154-156

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed.

In
`@extensions/implementation/src/main/scala/domino/extensions/implementation/DefaultExtensionService.scala`
around lines 63 - 65, The post-write telemetry calls (e.g.,
extensionPersister.create(...) followed by
extensionEventTracker.recordCreateExtension(...), and the similar
recordPatchExtension/recordDeleteExtension calls) must be made fail-open so
audit failures don't convert into API errors; wrap each extensionEventTracker.*
call in a try/catch that logs the telemetry failure (including exception
details) but does not rethrow, ensuring the successful persistence (returning
the extension) is not affected by telemetry exceptions.
```

</details>

```shell
#!/bin/bash
# Verify EventFormats includes serializers for new extension telemetry classes

EVENT_FORMAT_FILES=$(fd 'EventFormats.scala')
if [ -z "$EVENT_FORMAT_FILES" ]; then
  echo "No EventFormats.scala found"
  exit 0
fi

echo "$EVENT_FORMAT_FILES" | xargs -I{} rg -n \
  'CreateExtension|PatchExtension|DeleteExtension|PatchMountPointInfo|PatchTopBarInfo|PatchProjectSidebarInfo|PatchDatasetFileContextInfo' {}
```

**Add serialization formats to EventFormats.scala for new extension telemetry events.**

Lines 45-48 of events.scala explicitly require serialization support: "Events must be JSON serializable in order for their fields to be passed to mixpanel. You must add serialization logic to the telemetry EventFormats file."

The new extension event classes (CreateExtension, PatchExtension, DeleteExtension) and their helper case classes (PatchMountPointInfo, PatchTopBarInfo, PatchProjectSidebarInfo, PatchDatasetFileContextInfo) are missing from the TelemetryEventWrites pattern match in EventFormats.scala. Without these entries, these events will serialize to empty JSON, breaking telemetry and audit logging.

Add the following to EventFormats.scala:
1. Implicit formats for the helper case classes
2. Cases in TelemetryEventWrites for CreateExtension, PatchExtension, and DeleteExtension

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed.

In `@server/app/domino/server/lib/telemetry/events.scala` around lines 5725 -
5819, The new extension telemetry types (CreateExtension, PatchExtension,
DeleteExtension and helper case classes PatchMountPointInfo, PatchTopBarInfo,
PatchProjectSidebarInfo, PatchDatasetFileContextInfo) lack JSON serializers and
therefore serialize to empty objects; update EventFormats.scala by adding
implicit Format/Reads/Writes (or Format) instances for the four helper case
classes and then add pattern-match cases in TelemetryEventWrites to handle
CreateExtension, PatchExtension and DeleteExtension, returning JSON that
includes their fields (for PatchExtension include the nested changes and the
optional patch* fields and nested mount info); ensure the pattern uses the exact
case class names (CreateExtension, PatchExtension, DeleteExtension) and that the
implicit formats are in scope for the TelemetryEventWrites object.
```

</details>

any concerns with including `appName` like in other places

https://github.com/cerebrotech/domino/blob/827ab1408055847e96c312f0cc62af599adf4888/server/app/domino/server/user/domain/UserConsentEventFactory.scala#L32

My main concern with including it would be the additional performance overhead of an additional DB query to retrieve `appName` so I decided to only include the ID. But if it is important for auditing, I can make the changes.
yeah that's a reasonable concern but unfortunately the guidance from Matt was that we should always include a name for readability
I see, that makes sense. I'll make the changes, thanks!
Now updated to include app name.
